channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Top Biden agency issues blunt message to criminal immigrants after explosive data becomes liability for admin
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement are issuing a tough warning to noncitizens who commit felonies, telling them they forfeit the privilege to be in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As women seek open congressional seats in Maryland, reproductive rights are front and center
item.description: FREDERICK, Md. (AP) — In an election where the future of reproductive rights is on the ballot in Maryland and elsewhere across the country, the state’s all-male congressional delegation stands to gain an influx of women.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With little say over Robert Roberson's fate, Texas lawmakers take extraordinary steps to buy him more time
item.description: With Roberson’s options dwindling, a House panel used its bully pulpit to prove his case and excoriate the failures of the state’s junk science law.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other service workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Biden making quick trip to Germany before US election to discuss Ukraine and democracy with allies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has long believed diplomacy is about personal relationships — and he'll spend Friday in Berlin with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz as his time in office is on the cusp of ending.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FLASHBACK: Reaction to Harris' plagiarism story much more benign than for Biden during '88 election
item.description: Reactions to plagiarism accusations levied against Kamala Harris seem more benign than what Joe Biden faced in his presidential bid in 1988 after being accused of the same thing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With time running out, Harris keeps focus on 'blue wall' states
item.description: Kamala Harris is spending her week trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to pick ‘best’ Republicans for 2nd term Cabinet: Lutnick
item.description: Donald Trump is in the unusual position of not being backed by a number of members of his previous Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Harris Fox News interview draws sharp reactions on both sides
item.description: “If she wins, it will be because of that answer … which is ‘I’m not Joe Biden,’” columnist Chris Cillizza noted.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance says 'no,' he does not think Trump lost in 2020
item.description: Vance has faced repeated questions on the campaign trail about whether Trump lost in 2020. On Wednesday, he gave his most direct answer to date, answering "no" in response to a reporter's question.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Bakersfield Chamber of Commerce, DA Zimmer urge support for Prop 36
item.description: "Californians, whether they be independents, Democrats, or Republicans have had enough," said Kern County District Attorney Cynthia Zimmer.
item.dc:creator: Connor Dore, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris calls on Republican voters to put 'country first' as Trump woos Latino voters
item.description: WASHINGTON CROSSING, Pa. (AP) — Surrounded by more than 100 former Republican officeholders and officials, Democrat Kamala Harris urged GOP voters on Wednesday to put “country first” and abandon Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris, Bret Baier spar over immigration at outset of Fox News interview
item.description: Vice President Harris on Wednesday sparred at length with Fox News anchor Bret Baier over immigration in a rare sit-down with the network. Baier opened the interview by asking Harris how many immigrants had crossed the southern border during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgians flock to cast ballots as early in-person voting begins
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgians aren't waiting to vote, with what could be more than 10% of expected turnout casting ballots through Wednesday. More than 310,000 people voted on Tuesday, the first day of early in-person voting, with another 260,000 more making their choices through Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-ICE official warns Tren de Aragua has grown faster inside US than murderous rival gang: ‘Put them out now’
item.description: A former ICE field director is warning that Tren de Aragua, a violent Venezuelan gang, is establishing a presence in the United States and need to be stamped out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FEMA leader: Misinformation about hurricane operations must stop
item.description: "The ongoing spread of misinformation about operations is not OK," FEMA Administrator Deanne Criswell said.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Judge in Trump election case directs prosecutors to turn over info related to Pence documents probe
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge overseeing the election interference case against Donald Trump directed prosecutors Wednesday to search for and provide to the former president's lawyers any Justice Department information related to a separate investigation into Mike Pence's handling of classifie
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New poll shows Harris, Trump splitting 2 key states
item.description: With 20 days until Election Day, new polls show Trump leading Harris in battleground Georgia but the Democrat VP edging the Republican former president in purple North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris leading Trump by 5 points among likely voters: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris is leading former President Trump by 5 points nationally among likely voters, according to a Marist Poll survey released Wednesday. Harris and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), received 52 percent support, while Trump and his running mate, Sen.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris campaign hosts separated families ahead of Trump's Univision town hall
item.description: The Harris campaign has mostly centered its immigration and border security messaging on enforcement, drawing some criticism from advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany this week, Angola in December for visits delayed by Hurricane Milton
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is heading to Germany Thursday and will visit Angola the first week in December, rescheduling visits that were postponed so he could remain in Washington to monitor the federal response to Hurricane Milton as it struck Florida.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canceling subscriptions has to be as easy as signing up, FTC says in a new rule
item.description: The "click-to-cancel" rule, now finalized by the Federal Trade Commission, aims to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and memberships. Companies argue the agency overstepped its authority.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: Eric Adams welcomes new administration members amid wave of resignations
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some new faces are joining Mayor Eric Adams' administration as it works to stabilize City Hall after a series of high-level exits. A new city schools chancellor and a new deputy mayor for public safety are taking the reins.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Harris peppered with questions by Charlamagne tha God’s audience
item.description: Vice President Harris was peppered with questions Tuesday about her plan for Black Americans from radio host Charlamagne tha God and his listeners in an interview that’s part of a late-campaign media blitz before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Harris outreach to Black men: New policy, interviews, meetings
item.description: On the economic front, Harris intends to provide one million loans that would be fully forgivable of up to $20,000 to Black entrepreneurs.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Harris' Fox interview a bigger risk for Bret Baier: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly discusses the state of the race as Kamala Harris and Donald Trump work to energize key voter groups.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Geraldo Rivera backing Harris: Trump 'cannot be trusted to honor the Constitution'
item.description: Television personality Geraldo Rivera revealed Monday that he is backing Vice President Kamala Harris over former President Donald Trump in the election, now less than a month away.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: In divided St. Cloud, challengers hope to flip House seats
item.description: While many legislative districts in Minnesota are either solidly Republican or DFL, in the St. Cloud, voters are evenly split. Races for the two House seats that represent the region are among the most competitive in the state.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Harris slams Trump for suggesting military handle 'enemy from within' as they vie for Pennsylvania
item.description: Harris, at a rally in Pennsylvania, called Trump a serious threat to American democracy who is “out for unchecked power.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, WILL WEISSERT and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez, challenger Joe Kent face off in Washington 3rd District debate
item.description: One of the races that could determine the fate of Congress this election season happens to be the rematch race for Washington's 3rd District, and on Monday night, the candidates will debate on KOIN 6.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: One-on-one with Mississippi's candidates in 2024 US Senate race
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (WJTV) - U.S. Senator Roger Wicker (R-Miss.) and his Democratic opponent, Ty Pinkins, spoke to WJTV 12 News ahead of the November General Election. Wicker, a Pontotoc native, has been serving Mississippi in the U.S. Senate since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wright, NewsNation

item.title: With hunting party, Walz invites moderate male voters into the fold
item.description: Independent voter David Clayton joined Gov. Tim Walz at Saturday’s pheasant opener and says he’s sold on voting for the Harris-Walz ticket. He thinks others may be, too, but aren’t talking about it. “There are many, many MAGA-minded Americans who aren’t really so MAGA-minded.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Megan Burks, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Tim Scott mulls run to lead Senate Republicans' campaign arm
item.description: Sen. Tim Scott (R-S.C.) is seriously considering making a run to become chair of the National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC) in 2026, sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day to combat what he described as potential chaos from “the enemy from within” – a group that Trump said includes “radical left lunatics.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Shondra Summers-Armstrong discusses key issues in Las Vegas city council race
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) — Shondra Summers-Armstrong, currently serving as an Assemblywoman for District 6, is running for Las Vegas City Council, Ward 5.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
